task_description: Search for a hotel in London, open a specific property's details, review its facilities photos, and proceed to check room availability and prices.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows a wide white rounded search input box with gray placeholder text reading "输入城市/区域/酒店名称等" and a semitransparent red X centered inside the field, indicating the target is this search text field. In the full screen, this field is the leftmost control in a large booking search bar over a scenic mountain-lake background on an Agoda page with a large apology message in Chinese and date, guest, and blue search button controls to the right.
caption.think: The user likely wants to activate the destination search field so they can enter a city, area, or hotel name. This is a UI control in the main content area, and clicking it should place focus in the field.
caption.action: Click the city/area/hotel name search input field.
caption.expectation: The search field should become active with a text cursor, and it may open suggestions or allow typing a destination immediately.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped area shows the leftmost white rounded destination search field in the Agoda booking bar. Inside it, a magnifying-glass icon appears at the left and the text now reads "londo", indicating destination text is being entered into the active search box rather than selecting a result. Directly below, a white dropdown suggestion panel is open with multiple location suggestions in Chinese, including entries with star icons and hotel counts, and one row highlighted in blue. In the full screen, this search bar sits centered over a dimmed scenic mountain-and-lake background on an Agoda page with a large Chinese apology message, date selectors, a guest/room selector, and a blue search button to the right.
caption.think: The user is entering a destination name into the active location field, continuing to specify London for the hotel search. The typed text appears to be populating the search field and filtering suggestions below.
caption.action: Type "LONDON" into the destination search field, continuing/replacing the current partial text so the field contains the intended destination name.
caption.expectation: The search field should update to show the typed destination text, and the suggestion list below should refresh to London-related matches.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a blue-highlighted row in the open destination suggestion dropdown, with the semitransparent red X centered on that highlighted entry. The visible text on the row is the London suggestion in Chinese, reading "倫敦, 英國", with a small yellow tag beside it and a smaller category label below indicating it is a city. In the full screen, this dropdown is attached to the left destination search field in Agoda’s booking bar, where "london" is already typed; other location suggestions appear above and below, while date, guest, and the blue search button remain to the right over the dimmed mountain-lake background.
caption.think: The user wants to choose the highlighted London, United Kingdom destination from the autocomplete list rather than continue typing. This is a click on a list item UI control in the main page content, and it should select that suggestion.
caption.action: Click the blue-highlighted destination suggestion "倫敦, 英國" in the dropdown list.
caption.expectation: That destination entry should be selected, the dropdown should close or update, and the destination field should be filled with the chosen London location.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows Agoda’s date-picker calendar for April 2026. A semitransparent red X is centered directly on the day cell labeled "11", which sits immediately to the right of the already blue-highlighted day "10". This indicates the exact target is the calendar date cell for April 11, a clickable object within the calendar popup. In the full screen, the calendar dropdown is open beneath the booking bar on the Agoda page, with the destination field set to London in Chinese, date fields at the center, guest selector to the right, and a blue search button beside them.
caption.think: The user has already chosen April 10 as the check-in date and now wants to click April 11 as the check-out date, completing a one-night date range in the calendar.
caption.action: Click the calendar day cell "11" in the open date-picker.
caption.expectation: The date cell for April 11 should become selected as the end date, updating the date range; the calendar may close or the date fields should refresh to show the selected stay dates.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows Agoda’s date-picker calendar, and a semitransparent red X is centered on the blue-filled day cell labeled "13" in the April 2026 month view. This is a clickable calendar date cell, not a toolbar control or blank area. In the full-screen view, the Agoda booking form is open over a dimmed scenic background, with destination set to London in Chinese, the date popup showing April and May 2026, April 11 already shown in the check-in field, and the blue search button to the right.
caption.think: After selecting April 11 and then April 12, the user likely wants to adjust the date range by choosing April 13 as the check-out date, extending the stay by one more night. The target is the date cell itself in the calendar popup.
caption.action: Click the calendar day cell "13" in the April 2026 date-picker.
caption.expectation: The date selection should update immediately, with April 13 becoming the selected end date in the range; the calendar may remain open briefly or the booking date fields may refresh to reflect the new stay dates.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a large blue rectangular button with white Chinese text reading "搜出好價" and a semitransparent red X centered directly on that button. This is a clickable UI control, not a blank area or canvas object. In the full-screen view, the Agoda page is open in Chrome over a lake-and-mountain background, with the destination set to London, the date fields filled, a guest selector dropdown currently open below the occupancy field, and the same blue search button positioned at the right end of the booking bar.
caption.think: The user has already chosen destination and dates and is now clicking the Agoda search button to submit the hotel search with the current travel details.
caption.action: Click the blue "搜出好價" search button.
caption.expectation: The guest dropdown should close and the search should be submitted, leading to a results page or loading state showing hotel options for the selected London trip details.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a hotel result card with a semitransparent red X centered on the bold hotel title text "Belvilla Ilford Station Hotel, 4 min from Elizabeth line." This is a clickable object in the results list, specifically the hotel listing link rather than a blank area or toolbar control. In the full-screen view, Chrome is displaying Agoda search results for London, with filters on the left, a list of hotel cards in the center, and the same hotel card visible near the top of the results.
caption.think: After submitting the Agoda search, the user likely wants to open this specific hotel's details page by selecting the hotel listing from the search results.
caption.action: Click the hotel title "Belvilla Ilford Station Hotel, 4 min from Elizabeth line" in the results list.
caption.expectation: The hotel listing should become selected and Agoda should open that property's detail page or listing view for more information, pricing, and booking options.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the main hotel photo: a street-facing brick building with red trim, dormer windows, and storefronts at ground level. A semitransparent red X is centered on the building photo itself, over the middle facade area rather than on a button or toolbar control. This target is a clickable object on the page canvas, specifically the image in the hotel photo gallery. In the full-screen view, Chrome is on the Agoda property page for "Belvilla Ilford Station Hotel, 4 min from Elizabeth line," with a large gallery at the top-left, smaller thumbnail images to the right, a blue button on the photo saying "查看全部照片," and the hotel title and rating information below.
caption.think: The user likely wants to interact with the property's photo gallery to view the hotel images more closely, likely opening the selected image in a larger gallery or photo viewer.
caption.action: Click the main hotel photo in the image gallery.
caption.expectation: The image should become selected and Agoda should open a larger photo viewer or gallery view for the hotel's pictures.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a rounded light-gray category tab labeled "設施 (13)" with a semitransparent red X centered directly on it. This is a UI control in the photo viewer's tab bar, not an image tile or blank area. In the full-screen view, Agoda is open in Chrome with a modal-style hotel photo gallery overlay. Across the top of the gallery are category tabs including "全部 (33)", "客房 (8)", "酒店 (10)", "設施 (13)", and "膳食 (2)". A grid of hotel photos is visible below, and a right-side panel lists property amenities, confirming this is the gallery/filter interface.
caption.think: The user likely wants to switch the gallery from the currently shown category to the facilities-related photos by selecting the "設施 (13)" tab.
caption.action: Click the "設施 (13)" tab in the gallery category bar.
caption.expectation: The tab should become selected, and the photo grid should refresh to show the hotel's facilities photos.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in view shows no red X, indicating a scroll action rather than a click. The visible content is an Agoda photo gallery overlay with the category tabs across the top, where "設施 (13)" is selected in blue. Below it is a grid of facility-related photos, including several bathroom images with beige marble-like walls, a curved glass shower enclosure, sink vanities, a toilet, and some bedroom images. On the right side is a white information panel titled "人氣設施服務" listing amenities, with a prominent blue button at the bottom labeled "查看空房及價格". In the full-screen view, this gallery is open as a modal over the hotel page in Chrome, and the gallery content area appears scrollable with more photos extending below the visible area.
caption.think: The user is continuing to browse the facilities photo gallery after selecting the facilities tab, so they likely want to move downward within the modal to see additional facility photos and related content lower in the gallery.
caption.action: Scroll down within the Agoda photo gallery overlay to move the photo grid downward and reveal more facilities images below.
caption.expectation: The gallery should shift downward, showing new photos and lower content in the facilities section while keeping the modal open.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a large rounded blue button with white Chinese text reading "查看空房及價格" and a semitransparent red X centered directly on the text area of the button. This is a UI control, not an image or blank region. In the full-screen view, the Agoda hotel photo gallery overlay is still open with the "設施 (13)" tab selected, a grid of facility and room photos in the center, a white amenities panel on the right, and the same blue "查看空房及價格" button at the bottom of that right-side panel.
caption.think: The user likely wants to leave the gallery-focused browsing flow and move toward checking booking availability and pricing for this hotel by activating the prominent call-to-action button.
caption.action: Click the blue "查看空房及價格" button.
caption.expectation: The button should activate, likely closing or shifting focus away from the gallery overlay and taking the page to the room availability and pricing section.
